reasoning: Russian forces remain approximately 10.5 km away from Pokrovsk (as of mid-April 2025) and have to bypass or fight through surrounding fortified satellite towns like Myrnohrad before they can directly assault the city. Recent ISW reports from early June 2025 highlight that Russian forces made no confirmed advances in this direction, and in mid-May, Ukrainian forces even mounted localized counterattacks. Given Russia's historical advance rate of roughly 2-3.3 km per month in this sector, and the immense time it traditionally takes them to capture urban centers (like Bakhmut or Avdiivka), completing an advance and securing the city center (where St. Michael's Church is located) by the strict deadline of December 31, 2025, seems difficult. While the market was optimistic about capture on May 29 (58%), the lack of operational momentum in June and the ~6.5 months remaining lead me to estimate the probability somewhat lower, as taking a major urban agglomeration within this specific timeframe requires either a substantial acceleration in advance rates or a total collapse of local Ukrainian defenses.
